skill.name: openhue
skill.description: Control Philips Hue lights, rooms, and scenes through the OpenHue CLI.
skill.summary: Manage Philips Hue lighting from assistant-guided OpenHue CLI commands.
skill.author: aiskillstore

security_audit.summary: This official skill was reviewed with focus on marketplace readiness and minimal security confirmation. It documents OpenHue CLI commands that use local network access to a Hue Bridge, with no prompt injection or malicious behavior detected.

content.user_title: Control Philips Hue Lights with OpenHue
content.value_statement: Smart lighting commands can be slow when you need exact rooms, scenes, or bulb settings. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use OpenHue CLI commands accurately.
actual_capabilities: List Hue lights, rooms, and scenes through OpenHue get commands.
Turn individual lights or rooms on and off by name.
Set brightness levels for compatible lights and rooms from 0 to 100.
Adjust color temperature and RGB or named color values on capable bulbs.
Activate named scenes in a selected Hue room.
limitations: Requires OpenHue CLI installed and available in the shell.
Requires a Hue Bridge on the local network and first-run pairing.
Does not control non-Hue devices, HomeKit scenes, televisions, or thermostats.
Color changes only work on bulbs that support color.

use_cases.title: Adjust Home Lighting
use_cases.description: Turn lights on or off, dim rooms, and set comfortable color temperature for daily use.

use_cases.title: Prepare Workspaces
use_cases.description: Set bright, cool lighting in an office or studio before meetings, calls, or focused work.

use_cases.title: Run Scene-Based Routines
use_cases.description: Activate named scenes such as Relax or Concentrate without remembering exact CLI syntax.
use_cases.target_user: Assistant users who prefer text control

prompt_templates.title: Turn Lights On or Off
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn off the Bedroom lights with OpenHue. Confirm the target room before taking action.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner control for a single room

prompt_templates.title: Set Room Brightness
prompt_templates.prompt: Set the Office lights to 80 percent brightness with OpenHue. Keep the lights on.

prompt_templates.title: Activate a Scene
prompt_templates.prompt: Use OpenHue to activate the Concentrate scene in the Office room.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Lighting Routine
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an evening lighting routine for Bedroom and Living Room using OpenHue. Use warm, dim settings and explain each step before running commands.

output_examples.input: Turn the Bedroom Lamp on at 50 percent brightness.
output_examples.output: I will turn on Bedroom Lamp and set brightness to 50 percent using OpenHue.

output_examples.input: Set movie mode in the Living Room.
output_examples.output: I will dim the Living Room lights to a low level suitable for watching a movie.
I will list the scenes available from your Hue Bridge through OpenHue.

best_practices: Confirm the exact light, room, or scene name before changing lighting.
Use room commands for group changes and light commands for single bulbs.
Check bulb capability before requesting color or RGB changes.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for non-Hue smart devices or entertainment systems.
Do not assume color support on white-only Hue bulbs.
Do not run broad lighting changes without confirming the affected room or zone.

faq.question: What does this skill control?
faq.answer: It controls Philips Hue lights, rooms, and scenes through the OpenHue CLI.

faq.question: Does it require a Hue Bridge?
faq.answer: Yes. The Hue Bridge must be available on the local network.

faq.question: Can it control non-Hue devices?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for Philips Hue devices supported by OpenHue.

faq.question: Can it set colors?
faq.answer: Yes, when the target bulb supports color. White-only bulbs cannot use color settings.

faq.question: Does first use require setup?
faq.answer: Yes. First use may require pressing the Hue Bridge button to pair OpenHue.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
